It was said that a vow uttered under its silver glow would bend the world toward truth. To seal such a vow, the elders held a public oath during Festival of Echoes. The speaker stood on cedar plank and named the covenant before all: to guard the Borderlands from deceit and to keep honesty within every heart.

To bind the vow more firmly, the speaker undertook a costly act: they cast their cherished coin into the Flame of Remembering, a fire that consumed pride. The coin vanished, but the act remained as a weight in the memory of the community. From that day people spoke of two duties: keep the word spoken and guard the flame within.

Temptation arrived as whispers of ease and expedience. Some dreamed of breaking the vow for quick gain, to ease suffering or to avoid blame. Yet the Moon-Anchor reflected not punishment, but consequence: when a single promise is broken, the land grows thinner, trust wanes, and generations must labor twice to mend a thread.

Over time, vow-keeping grew, unnoticeably, into the gravity of life itself.
